name: HADACA3_PDAC_MultiOmics_Deconvolution_Benchmark
description: A benchmark dataset for cell-type deconvolution in pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma (PDAC), released as part of the HADACA3 challenge. Includes RNA expression mixtures, DNA methylation mixtures, and paired ground-truth cell-type proportions for 24 pseudo-samples across 9 simulation scenarios (in silico Dirichlet with various dependency/source structures, pseudobulk, in vitro, in vivo), each replicated twice. Reference profiles are provided for bulk RNA-seq, methylation arrays, and single-cell RNA-seq (three independent PDAC cohorts: baron, peng, raghavan). Cell types: endothelial (endo), fibroblast (fibro), immune, classical PDAC (classic), basal-like PDAC (basal). Helper scripts for reading and writing all HDF5 files are provided in Python (data_processing.py, using h5py/anndata) and R (data_processing.R, using rhdf5/Matrix) at https://github.com/bioinfo-LIG/hadaca3_framework/tree/main/utils.

distribution.name: ref
distribution.description: Reference profiles: bulk RNA-seq (5 cell types × 15 908 genes), DNA methylation (5 cell types × 23 724 CpG sites), and three single-cell RNA-seq cohorts (baron 551 cells, peng 4 974 cells, raghavan 4 953 cells). Sparse matrices stored in CSC format (data/indices/indptr/shape).

distribution.name: mixes1_insilicodirichletCopule
distribution.description: RNA+methylation mixture file – replicate 1. Scenario: In silico Dirichlet sampling with copula-based inter-cell-type dependency. Contains /mix_met/data (24×23724 float64), /mix_rna/data (15908 float64), plus sample/feature labels.

distribution.name: groundtruth1_insilicodirichletCopule
distribution.description: Ground-truth cell-type proportions – replicate 1. Scenario: In silico Dirichlet sampling with copula-based inter-cell-type dependency. Contains /groundtruth/data (24×2 float64) for 'classic' and 'basal' PDAC subtypes.

distribution.name: mixes2_insilicodirichletCopule
distribution.description: RNA+methylation mixture file – replicate 2. Scenario: In silico Dirichlet sampling with copula-based inter-cell-type dependency. Contains /mix_met/data (24×23724 float64), /mix_rna/data (15908 float64), plus sample/feature labels.

distribution.name: groundtruth2_insilicodirichletCopule
distribution.description: Ground-truth cell-type proportions – replicate 2. Scenario: In silico Dirichlet sampling with copula-based inter-cell-type dependency. Contains /groundtruth/data (24×2 float64) for 'classic' and 'basal' PDAC subtypes.

recordSet.name: reference_bulkRNA
recordSet.description: Bulk RNA-seq reference profiles for 5 PDAC cell types (endo, fibro, immune, classic, basal) across 15 908 genes (float64, units ~ CPM/TPM).

field.name: cell_types
field.description: Cell-type labels: endo, fibro, immune, classic, basal. Shape (5,) HDF5 path: /ref_bulkRNA/cell_types.

field.name: genes
field.description: HGNC gene symbols. Shape (15908,) HDF5 path: /ref_bulkRNA/genes.

field.name: data
field.description: Expression matrix. Shape (5, 15908), float64. Row order matches cell_types; column order matches genes HDF5 path: /ref_bulkRNA/data.

recordSet.name: reference_methylation
recordSet.description: DNA methylation reference profiles for 5 PDAC cell types across 23 724 CpG sites (Illumina beta-values, float64).

field.name: cell_types
field.description: Cell-type labels: endo, fibro, immune, classic, basal. Shape (5,) HDF5 path: /ref_met/cell_types.

field.name: CpG_sites
field.description: Illumina cg-IDs (e.g. cg00002426). Shape (23724,) HDF5 path: /ref_met/CpG_sites.

field.name: data
field.description: Beta-value matrix. Shape (5, 23724), float64. Row order matches cell_types; column order matches CpG_sites HDF5 path: /ref_met/data.

recordSet.name: reference_scRNA_baron
recordSet.description: Single-cell RNA-seq reference from the Baron cohort: 551 cells × 20125 genes stored as a sparse CSC matrix (982,828 non-zero entries). Reconstruct with scipy.sparse.csc_matrix((data, indices, indptr), shape=shape).

field.name: cell
field.description: Cell barcodes/identifiers. Shape (551,) HDF5 path: /ref_scRNA/ref_sc_baron/cell.

field.name: genes
field.description: Gene symbols. Shape (20125,) HDF5 path: /ref_scRNA/ref_sc_baron/genes.

field.name: meta
field.description: Per-cell metadata (cell-type annotation). Shape (551,) HDF5 path: /ref_scRNA/ref_sc_baron/meta.

field.name: data
field.description: Non-zero values of the CSC sparse matrix. Shape (982828,), float HDF5 path: /ref_scRNA/ref_sc_baron/data.

field.name: indices
field.description: Row indices of non-zero values (CSC). Shape (982828,), int HDF5 path: /ref_scRNA/ref_sc_baron/indices.

field.name: indptr
field.description: Column pointer array (CSC). Shape (552,), int HDF5 path: /ref_scRNA/ref_sc_baron/indptr.

field.name: shape
field.description: Dense matrix dimensions [n_genes, n_cells]. Shape (2,), int HDF5 path: /ref_scRNA/ref_sc_baron/shape.

recordSet.name: reference_scRNA_peng
recordSet.description: Single-cell RNA-seq reference from the Peng cohort: 4974 cells × 24005 genes stored as a sparse CSC matrix (12,399,241 non-zero entries). Reconstruct with scipy.sparse.csc_matrix((data, indices, indptr), shape=shape).

field.name: cell
field.description: Cell barcodes/identifiers. Shape (4974,) HDF5 path: /ref_scRNA/ref_sc_peng/cell.

field.name: genes
field.description: Gene symbols. Shape (24005,) HDF5 path: /ref_scRNA/ref_sc_peng/genes.

field.name: meta
field.description: Per-cell metadata (cell-type annotation). Shape (4974,) HDF5 path: /ref_scRNA/ref_sc_peng/meta.

field.name: data
field.description: Non-zero values of the CSC sparse matrix. Shape (12399241,), float HDF5 path: /ref_scRNA/ref_sc_peng/data.

field.name: indices
field.description: Row indices of non-zero values (CSC). Shape (12399241,), int HDF5 path: /ref_scRNA/ref_sc_peng/indices.

field.name: indptr
field.description: Column pointer array (CSC). Shape (4975,), int HDF5 path: /ref_scRNA/ref_sc_peng/indptr.

field.name: shape
field.description: Dense matrix dimensions [n_genes, n_cells]. Shape (2,), int HDF5 path: /ref_scRNA/ref_sc_peng/shape.

recordSet.name: reference_scRNA_raghavan
recordSet.description: Single-cell RNA-seq reference from the Raghavan cohort: 4953 cells × 18710 genes stored as a sparse CSC matrix (12,263,755 non-zero entries). Reconstruct with scipy.sparse.csc_matrix((data, indices, indptr), shape=shape).

field.name: cell
field.description: Cell barcodes/identifiers. Shape (4953,) HDF5 path: /ref_scRNA/ref_sc_raghavan/cell.

field.name: genes
field.description: Gene symbols. Shape (18710,) HDF5 path: /ref_scRNA/ref_sc_raghavan/genes.

field.name: meta
field.description: Per-cell metadata (cell-type annotation). Shape (4953,) HDF5 path: /ref_scRNA/ref_sc_raghavan/meta.

field.name: data
field.description: Non-zero values of the CSC sparse matrix. Shape (12263755,), float HDF5 path: /ref_scRNA/ref_sc_raghavan/data.

field.name: indices
field.description: Row indices of non-zero values (CSC). Shape (12263755,), int HDF5 path: /ref_scRNA/ref_sc_raghavan/indices.

field.name: indptr
field.description: Column pointer array (CSC). Shape (4954,), int HDF5 path: /ref_scRNA/ref_sc_raghavan/indptr.

field.name: shape
field.description: Dense matrix dimensions [n_genes, n_cells]. Shape (2,), int HDF5 path: /ref_scRNA/ref_sc_raghavan/shape.

recordSet.name: mixtures_insilicodirichletCopule_rep1
recordSet.description: RNA + methylation mixtures, scenario 'insilicodirichletCopule', replicate 1. In silico Dirichlet with copula-based dependency. 24 samples × 23724 CpG sites (methylation) and 15908 genes (RNA).

field.name: met_samples
field.description: Sample IDs for methylation block. Shape (24,). HDF5 path: /mix_met/samples.

field.name: CpG_sites
field.description: Illumina cg-IDs. Shape (23724,). HDF5 path: /mix_met/CpG_sites.

field.name: met_data
field.description: Beta-value methylation matrix. Shape (24, 23724), float64. HDF5 path: /mix_met/data.

field.name: rna_samples
field.description: Sample IDs for RNA block. Shape (24,). HDF5 path: /mix_rna/samples.

field.name: rna_genes
field.description: Gene symbols. Shape (15908,). HDF5 path: /mix_rna/genes.

field.name: rna_data
field.description: Bulk RNA expression mixture. Shape (15908,), float64. HDF5 path: /mix_rna/data.

recordSet.name: groundtruth_insilicodirichletCopule_rep1
recordSet.description: Ground-truth proportions, scenario 'insilicodirichletCopule', replicate 1. In silico Dirichlet with copula-based dependency. 24 samples × 2 PDAC subtypes (classic, basal).

field.name: samples
field.description: Sample IDs. Shape (24,). HDF5 path: /groundtruth/samples.

field.name: column_labels
field.description: Column labels: classic and basal. Shape (2,). HDF5 path: /groundtruth/genes.

field.name: data
field.description: Proportion matrix. Shape (24, 2), float64. Columns: classic, basal. HDF5 path: /groundtruth/data.

cr:DataTransformation.name: Read HDF5 files with Python
cr:DataTransformation.description: Load any dataset file using read_hdf5(path) from data_processing.py. Returns a dict whose keys match the HDF5 top-level groups: ref_bulkRNA and ref_met as pandas DataFrames (genes/CpG_sites × cell_types); ref_scRNA as a dict of AnnData objects (one per cohort: baron, peng, raghavan), sparse matrix reconstructed as csc_matrix((data, indices, indptr), shape=shape).T; mix_rna and mix_met as pandas DataFrames (features × samples). Use write_global_hdf5(path, data_list) to write back.

cr:DataTransformation.name: Read HDF5 files with R
cr:DataTransformation.description: Load any dataset file using read_hdf5(path) from data_processing.R. Returns a named list matching the HDF5 top-level groups: ref_bulkRNA and ref_met as R matrices (genes/CpG_sites as rownames, cell types as colnames); ref_scRNA as a named list of list(counts=dgCMatrix, metadata=data.frame) per cohort; mix_rna and mix_met as matrices (features as rownames, samples as colnames). Use write_global_hdf5(path, data_list) to write back.

sc:SoftwareSourceCode.name: data_processing.py
sc:SoftwareSourceCode.description: Python helper library for reading and writing all HDF5 files in this dataset. Main public functions: read_hdf5(path) – generic loader that returns a dict matching the HDF5 top-level groups (ref_bulkRNA / ref_met as pandas DataFrames indexed by genes/CpG_sites, ref_scRNA as AnnData objects reconstructed via scipy.sparse.csc_matrix((data, indices, indptr), shape=shape).T, mix_rna / mix_met as pandas DataFrames); write_global_hdf5(path, data_list) – generic writer for all file types. Requires: h5py, numpy, pandas, scipy, anndata.

sc:SoftwareSourceCode.name: data_processing.R
sc:SoftwareSourceCode.description: R helper library for reading and writing all HDF5 files in this dataset. Main public functions: read_hdf5(path) – generic loader returning a named list; ref_bulkRNA / ref_met as R matrices with rownames (genes / CpG_sites) and colnames (cell types / samples), ref_scRNA as list(counts=dgCMatrix, metadata=data.frame) per cohort, mix_rna / mix_met as matrices with rownames and colnames; write_global_hdf5(path, data_list) – generic writer for all file types. Requires: rhdf5, Matrix.

citeAs: HADACA3 PDAC MultiOmics Deconvolution Benchmark Datasets. Universite Grenoble Alpes. https://github.com/bioinfo-LIG/hadaca3_framework
rai:dataLimitations: This dataset is focused on pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma (PDAC) and may not generalize to other cancer types or tissues with different cellular compositions. The benchmark relies on reference-based deconvolution, making performance dependent on the quality and representativeness of the reference profiles. Simulated datasets, while designed to cover diverse scenarios, may not fully capture complex biological dependencies such as inter-modality correlations or unknown cell states. This dataset is not recommended for direct clinical decision-making or for training models intended for deployment without further validation on independent real-world data.
rai:dataBiases: The dataset may reflect biases inherent to the source data. Bulk and single-cell reference profiles are derived from publicly available datasets, which may over-represent specific experimental conditions, populations, or sample preparation protocols. The focus on PDAC limits diversity in tissue types and biological contexts.
rai:personalSensitiveInformation: The dataset does not contain directly identifiable personal information. It includes molecular profiles (RNA-seq and DNA methylation) derived from human samples, which may be considered sensitive biological data. All data originate from publicly available and previously consented datasets or are provided in aggregated or processed form. No demographic attributes such as age, gender, or socio-economic status are included in this benchmark.
rai:dataUseCases: This dataset is designed to evaluate computational methods for bulk tissue deconvolution and multi-omic data integration. It provides a controlled benchmark for assessing accuracy, robustness, and methodological trade-offs under diverse simulated and real-world conditions. Valid use cases include benchmarking, method development, and reproducible research in computational biology. The dataset is not intended for clinical applications, causal inference, or direct biological interpretation without further validation. It is also not designed for fairness auditing across demographic groups, as such information is not available.
rai:dataSocialImpact: This dataset can support the development of improved computational tools for understanding tissue composition, with potential applications in biomedical research and precision medicine. However, misuse or over-interpretation of benchmark results may lead to incorrect conclusions about method performance in real clinical settings.

prov:wasGeneratedBy.dataCollection: Publicly available bulk RNA-seq, DNA methylation, and single-cell RNA-seq datasets were retrieved from repositories such as GEO and GTEx. These datasets provide reference profiles for distinct cell types.
prov:wasGeneratedBy.preprocessing: RNA-seq data were normalized (e.g., counts per million or similar approaches), and DNA methylation data were processed as beta values. Feature selection was applied to retain shared features across datasets. Reference profiles were constructed by aggregating samples per cell type.
prov:wasGeneratedBy.simulation: In silico datasets were generated using a linear mixture model combining reference profiles with sampled cell-type proportions (Dirichlet distribution). Multiple noise models were applied independently to RNA and DNAm modalities, including Gaussian, chi-squared, EM-based, and copula-based noise. A pseudo-bulk dataset was generated by aggregating simulated single-cell profiles.
prov:wasGeneratedBy.annotation: Ground-truth cell-type proportions are known for simulated and in vitro datasets. For in vivo data, proportions were estimated using histology-based methods. No manual annotation by human labelers was performed as part of this benchmark.
prov:wasGeneratedBy.pipeline: All processing steps were implemented in a reproducible computational pipeline.
